Line spacing is comfortable, and there is clear separation between functional bands (filters/summary) and informational content (cards). No overly large whitespace zones are introduced; spacing uses the 8px base scale, with major separations at 24–32px and minor internal separations at 8–16px.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow maintains an F-pattern. After users scan the existing site header (school logo, board logo, nav, banner, tabs) and land on the breadcrumb and H1 in the top-left of the content column, their eye naturally drops to the Summer Camps filter bar as the next horizontal band. Within that band, the eye moves left to right across the filter controls and the search executor. Next, attention shifts downward to the summary text on the left and the sort control on the right, then continues vertically into the stacked camp cards. Within each card, the eye starts at the top-left camp title and flows across to the right-aligned key facts, then drops down to any secondary details and the call-to-action link or button.

layout.overall_description: Beneath the existing institutional header—the utility bar, blue global navigation, grey banner, and tab strip—the main Summer Camps content sits in a centered, fixed-width column. At the top of this column, the breadcrumb and a large 'Summer Camps' H1 anchor the page, optionally followed by a short line or two of introductory text. About 24–32px below this header area, a light, horizontal filter band spans the entire width of the content column. Within this band, neatly aligned dropdowns for month, daily schedule, and weekly fee sit in a row, left-aligned, with a right-aligned search/update button at the end. Another 16–24px down, a slim summary row states how many camps match the current filters on the left, while a compact sort control aligns to the right on the same baseline, forming a simple, balanced strip. Below that, the main content body is a vertical column of full-width camp session cards, each a bordered, flat white rectangle with the camp name and description on the left and structured key facts on the right. Cards are stacked with consistent vertical spacing, creating a tidy rhythm down the page. If no camps match, this list area is replaced by a centered, gently framed empty-state message aligned within the same column. Overall, the page reads as a sequence of horizontal bands and stacked cards, with clear hierarchy, practical spacing, and a conservative blue-accented look that matches the rest of the school site.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component does not render a visible UI element. Conceptually, it sits at the top of the Summer Camps page logic, wiring the filter bar, sort control, and results list to the browser URL. From a layout standpoint, it occupies no physical space in the content column and does not affect spacing between visible components; the user instead experiences its impact as the page reflecting filter/sort state when loading or sharing URLs.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, non-spatial behavior only. It is effectively 'fixed' to the page lifecycle rather than any scroll position, with no scrolling, sticky, or floating behavior.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: This component underpins the camp filter bar by loading available months, schedules, and fee ranges. Visually, when active, it can manifest as a subtle loading state inside the filter bar: each filter control area (month, schedule, fee) shows a greyed-out placeholder or skeleton in the exact positions where the actual dropdowns will appear. These placeholders are left-aligned within the filter bar and match the eventual control widths (each occupying roughly 25–30% of the content column, with 16px horizontal gaps). While loading, the overall filter bar still occupies the same vertical height—around 72–96px of total height including padding—preserving the layout so content below does not shift.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Occupies the same space as the filter controls at the top of the results area. It is not sticky; it scrolls naturally with the page. When data loading completes, its skeleton placeholders are replaced in-place by the functional filter controls without altering the bar's size.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp-filter-bar forms a distinct horizontal band directly beneath the page’s Summer Camps header (breadcrumb, H1, and any short intro). After approximately 24–32px of vertical whitespace below the H1/intro, a full-width bar appears, spanning nearly the entire content column (about 90–100% of the 1080–1200px width, with the site’s standard inner left/right padding of 24–32px). The bar has a light background (subtly contrasted from the page background) and a thin top or bottom border to echo the conservative, tabbed styling seen in the mockup. Inside, the controls are arranged in one or two rows depending on screen width. On a standard desktop width, three filter controls—Month, Daily Schedule, and Weekly Fee—are placed in a single row from left to right. Each control behaves like a form field or pill-shaped select, each occupying roughly 25–30% of the content width, separated by 16px horizontal gaps. On the far right of the same row, aligned to the right edge of the content column, sits the search executor button, slightly narrower (about 120–160px wide) and vertically centered with the inputs. Vertical padding of 12–16px above and below the controls gives the bar a compact but comfortable presence. On narrower screens, the filters can stack into two rows, but they remain left-aligned within this band, with the search button dropping to the row beneath the filters and right-aligned. This band is clearly perceived as the main interaction zone for configuring which camps are shown.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The filter bar scrolls with the page and is not sticky. It always sits directly above the results summary/sort row. When filters are changed, the bar remains in place while the underlying results list updates, preserving its height and position to avoid layout jumps.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp-sort-control appears as a compact dropdown or segmented control in the same horizontal row as the results summary, just below the filter bar. About 16–24px below the bottom edge of the filter bar, a low-height strip runs across the width of the content column. On the left side of this strip is the camp-results-summary text; the sort control is right-aligned within the same row, anchored to the right margin of the content column. The control itself is modest in size—roughly 160–220px wide—matching the height of a standard input (about 32–40px) and with 8–12px internal horizontal padding. There is 8–16px of vertical padding above and below this row so it feels like a thin, unobtrusive band rather than a heavy bar. The visual style is understated: simple flat field or dropdown with a chevron icon, aligning typographically with other controls. This placement allows users to glance at the summary on the left and immediately discover the sort options on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: It is inline with the results summary, not sticky, and scrolls with the page. It flexes with the column width: always hugging the right edge, while its width remains fixed or minimally responsive so that it does not dominate the row.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp-search-executor is a primary action button integrated into the lower-right of the filter bar. On desktop, it sits in the same horizontal row as the Month, Daily Schedule, and Weekly Fee controls, aligned flush to the right edge of the content column. It is slightly more visually prominent than the filters, using the school’s strong blue branding as a solid background, with white label text like 'Update results' or 'Apply filters'. The button’s width is around 120–160px, with 16–20px horizontal padding and 10–12px vertical padding, matching the height of the nearby filter fields. There is a modest 16px gap between the last filter control and the button if they share the same row. On narrower screens where the filters wrap, the button drops to the next line and remains right-aligned, with about 8–16px of vertical spacing above it to separate it from the row of filters. The button’s placement at the right end of the filter band reinforces the left-to-right interaction flow: choose filters from left to right, then confirm on the right.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This button lives inside the camp-filter-bar and moves with that bar as the page scrolls. It does not become sticky. When filters are adjusted, triggering this button refreshes the results while the layout of the filter bar itself remains fixed, avoiding any positional shift.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp-results-summary appears as left-aligned text in a slim information row immediately beneath the filter bar. After a vertical gap of about 16–24px below the filter band, this row begins, spanning the full width of the content column. The summary text—e.g., 'Showing 8 summer camps for July–August'—sits on the left, aligned with the main content column’s left margin. The font size matches body text or slightly emphasized body (e.g., 14–16px) with a semi-bold weight to distinguish it from paragraph copy. The row has 8–12px of vertical padding and optionally a light bottom border to subtly separate it from the camp cards below. On the right-hand side of this same row, aligned to the right margin, sits the camp-sort-control, creating a balanced left/right composition. The band itself is low-profile and does not use a heavy colored background, maintaining the conservative, information-first feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: It scrolls with the rest of the content and is always positioned directly above the results list (or empty state). The height of this row remains consistent to avoid layout jumps as the number of results changes.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp-results-list occupies the main body of the page beneath the summary/sort row. About 16–24px below the results summary band, the list begins, aligned to the same left and right margins as the rest of the content column. It consists of a vertical stack of camp-session-card components, each taking up nearly the full width of the column (around 90–100% of available width). The spacing between individual cards is consistent, typically a 16–24px vertical gap. There is an additional 24–32px of padding at the bottom of the list before reaching the page footer region, maintaining the breathing room characteristic of the mockup. When a substantial number of camps is returned, users can scroll down through this column of cards; no multi-column grid or masonry pattern is used, reinforcing the linear, information-oriented layout. The list maintains moderate density: several cards are visible within the viewport on a typical laptop, but each card has enough padding to remain legible.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: The list scrolls naturally within the main page flow. It expands vertically according to the number of results. When there are no results, this area is replaced entirely by the camp-empty-state-message, occupying the same vertical space at the top of the list region.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: Each camp-session-card appears as a flat, white rectangular panel with a subtle border (or thin outline) and no heavy shadows, reflecting the institutional, low-decoration style. The card spans almost the full width of the content column (about 90–100%), with internal padding of 16–24px on all sides. At the top-left of the card, the camp name is presented as a strong, left-aligned heading (e.g., H3), with 4–8px of space below it. Directly under the heading, a short one- or two-line description or tagline appears, followed by 8–12px of vertical spacing. Beneath this, the card is visually structured into two conceptual columns: on the left two-thirds, more detailed text (a brief overview or key highlights) is arranged in tidy paragraphs or a short bullet list; on the right one-third, a compact facts block lists the key attributes such as Month(s), Daily Schedule, Weekly Fee, and Grades. These facts can be arranged as a simple vertical list with labels in a lighter weight and values in a stronger weight, right- or left-aligned within that right-hand segment. At the bottom-right of the card, aligned beneath the facts or in line with them, a small 'View details' link or button appears, giving users a clear navigation affordance to a more detailed camp page. Vertical spacing within the card adheres to the 8–16px increments, ensuring a clear typographic hierarchy without feeling airy. Multiple cards stacked one after another reinforce the sense of a controlled, text-heavy but organized list.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Each card is a block-level element within the results list, stacking vertically with consistent spacing. Cards are not interactive in terms of layout (no expansion, no floating), though the entire card or just the 'View details' area may be clickable. They scroll with the page and do not change size or position dynamically, maintaining a stable reading rhythm.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When no camp sessions match the current filters, the camp-empty-state-message replaces the results list area beneath the summary/sort row. About 24–32px below the results summary band, a centered message block appears within the content column. This block can be a simple, lightly outlined box or just a vertical stack of an icon and text, constrained to about 50–60% of the column width and centered horizontally. The text is left-aligned within this narrower block, maintaining alignment with other content typography, and spaced with 8–12px between headline and explanatory copy. There is generous vertical padding—around 24–32px above and below the text—to create a clear, calm state. The message might read, for example, 'No camps match your filters' with a smaller line suggesting adjustments to filters. The overall treatment remains understated (greys and neutral tones), aligning with the site’s conservative design, while the central placement draws the eye to the status and next steps.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: This message is conditionally rendered in the same spatial region as the results list. It is centered within the content column and scrolls normally with the page. When results become available, it is removed and replaced by the stacked camp-session-card list without affecting surrounding sections like the filter bar or summary row.

name: Summer Camp Detail
title: Summer Camp Session Detail
description: Displays detailed information for a selected summer camp session based on the campSessionId URL parameter and provides a parent interest form that submits via submitCampInterestForm.

core_features: Read the campSessionId from the URL query string and load the corresponding summer camp session using getSummerCampSessionDetail.
Display full session details including session label, date range, daily schedule, location, weekly fee, activities summary, eligibility information, and active/inactive status.
Provide a parent/guardian interest form tied to the loaded session, collecting child name, upcoming grade, guardian name, phone number, optional email, and preferred session label.
Pre-fill and lock the preferred session label in the form to match the loaded session’s session_label so the submission is always associated with the correct session week.
Validate required form fields before submission and display inline validation errors for missing or invalid entries.
Submit the interest form using submitCampInterestForm with parameters derived from the URL (campSessionId), form fields, and loaded session details, then handle loading, success, and error states.
Show a confirmation state when the interest request has been successfully submitted, including any status and message returned by submitCampInterestForm.
Provide a navigational control to return to summer_camps.html, passing the session’s month as a URL parameter when available.
user_workflows:
View camp session details:
1) User opens camp_detail.html with a campSessionId parameter in the URL.
2) Page reads campSessionId from the query string.
3) Page calls getSummerCampSessionDetail(campSessionId).
4) While data is loading, a loading state is shown and the detail and form components are disabled.
5) On success, the page displays the session’s name, date range, daily schedule, fee, activities summary, location, eligibility details, and active status.
6) If the session is marked inactive, the page displays an informational notice and disables the interest form.

Submit an interest request for a camp session:
1) After session details load successfully, the interest form becomes available.
2) The form automatically sets the preferred session label field to the loaded session’s session_label and prevents editing of this value.
3) User enters child_name, selects upcoming_grade, enters guardian_name, phone_number, and optionally email.
4) On clicking the form submit button, the form runs client-side validation for required fields and basic formatting (e.g., phone is non-empty; email, if present, matches a valid pattern).
5) If validation fails, the form highlights errors and does not call submitCampInterestForm.
6) If validation passes, the page calls submitCampInterestForm with campSessionId from the URL, childName, upcomingGrade, guardianName, phoneNumber, email, and preferredSessionLabel from the loaded session.
7) While the request is in progress, the submit button shows a submitting state and is disabled to prevent duplicate submissions.
8) On success (success=true), the page displays a confirmation state including any message, hides or disables the form, and stores the returned camp_interest_submission_id and submitted_at for display.
9) On error (success=false or network error), the page displays an error message, keeps the form data in place, and allows the user to correct fields or retry.

Navigate back to the summer camps list:
1) After viewing details or submitting the interest form, the user clicks the Back to Summer Camps control.
2) If the session detail has loaded and includes a month value, the page builds a URL to summer_camps.html with ?month set to the session’s month.
3) If the session detail is not available or has no month, the page navigates to summer_camps.html without additional query parameters.
4) The browser then loads summer_camps.html, which can use the month parameter to display the appropriate month of camp sessions.
interactions: Load session details on page initialization: On page load or when the campSessionId URL parameter changes, the data loader component triggers getSummerCampSessionDetail(campSessionId). The result updates the session detail view and the interest form’s default values.

Handle missing or invalid campSessionId: If campSessionId is missing from the URL, or if getSummerCampSessionDetail returns an error or no session, the page shows an error state and disables the interest form while still providing a Back to Summer Camps navigation option.

Back to Summer Camps click: When the user clicks the back navigation control, the page reads the loaded session’s month (if available) and performs a client-side redirect to summer_camps.html, appending ?month=<session.month> to the URL. If month is not available, it redirects to summer_camps.html without query parameters.

Form field input and validation: As the user types or changes values in the interest form fields, the form component updates its internal state, clears field-level errors for the changed field, and can perform basic live validation (e.g., required field checks).

Form submission: When the user submits the form, the form component validates all required fields, and if valid, calls submitCampInterestForm with the combined data from URL, loaded session, and form inputs. The component manages a submitting state to prevent multiple submissions.

Submission success handling: On a successful response from submitCampInterestForm, the form component stores success, camp_interest_submission_id, status, submitted_at, and message. It then displays a confirmation banner/state and can disable or hide the form inputs to indicate completion.

Submission error handling: If submitCampInterestForm fails or returns success=false, the form component shows an error message, may map message to a general form error, and re-enables the submit button so the user can retry without losing entered data.

Inactive session handling: If the loaded SummerCampSession has is_active=false, an inactivity notice is displayed and the form submit button (or entire form) is disabled, preventing submission for closed or inactive sessions.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page’s primary state is driven by the campSessionId URL parameter and the responses from getSummerCampSessionDetail and submitCampInterestForm. On initial load, the page reads campSessionId from the query string (e.g., camp_detail.html?campSessionId=...). If campSessionId is present, the page enters a loading state and calls getSummerCampSessionDetail(campSessionId). The session detail state can be one of: loading, loaded, or error. When loaded, the SummerCampSession data object is stored in memory and used to render all detail components and to populate the interest form’s non-editable preferredSessionLabel from session_label, as well as the month used for back navigation. If the detail load fails or campSessionId is missing, the state is set to error and the interest form remains disabled. The interest form has its own sub-state: idle (ready for input), validating (momentary during client-side validation), submitting (after validation when calling submitCampInterestForm), success (after a successful submission), and error (if submission fails). The form’s ability to submit is gated by both the session detail state (must be loaded) and the session’s is_active flag (must be true). After a successful submission, the form state transitions to success, the returned submission metadata is stored, and form inputs can be locked to prevent additional submissions for the same session within the same page view. Navigation state is handled exclusively through URL parameters: campSessionId controls which session’s details are shown on this page, and the Back to Summer Camps navigation writes the month value from the loaded session into the URL for summer_camps.html as a month query parameter. No state is persisted via localStorage; all state is in memory and tied to the current page load and URL parameters.

components.functionality: On page initialization, read the campSessionId parameter from the URL query string and call getSummerCampSessionDetail(campSessionId). Manage loading, success, and error states, and provide the loaded SummerCampSession data (or error information) to child components.

Display the primary identifying information for the loaded camp session, including the session name and session_label, and optionally indicate active/inactive status. It updates whenever the loader provides new session data.
Show the session’s date range (start_date to end_date) and daily schedule (daily_start_time and daily_end_time). This provides parents with clear timing information for the selected camp session.
Display the weekly fee and currency for the camp session so parents can see the cost. The panel updates whenever the loaded SummerCampSession changes.

Display the location of the camp session, based on the location field from the loaded SummerCampSession. If location is absent, the panel can be hidden or show a generic notice based on configuration.

Show grade eligibility information for the session using eligible_grades and eligible_grade_labels to communicate which students may attend. This helps parents determine if their child’s upcoming grade is a match.
Display activities_summary from the loaded SummerCampSession so parents can understand the types of activities included in the session.

If the loaded SummerCampSession has is_active set to false, display a notice that the session is not currently accepting interest submissions and signal to the form component that it should be disabled.

Collect parent/guardian interest details for the loaded camp session and submit them through submitCampInterestForm. The form fields include child_name, upcoming_grade, guardian_name, phone_number, email (optional), and a non-editable preferred_session_label pre-filled from the session’s session_label. The form validates required fields, manages submitting and error states, and disables itself when the session is inactive or when a submission has succeeded.
Display form-level and field-level validation errors generated by the camp-interest-form component when required data is missing or incorrectly formatted.

Show a confirmation state after a successful call to submitCampInterestForm, including status, submitted_at, and any message returned. It becomes visible when the form component transitions to a success state.
Display an error message when submitCampInterestForm fails or returns success=false, helping users understand that their interest request was not recorded and that they may need to retry.

Provide a control that takes the user back to summer_camps.html. When the loaded SummerCampSession includes a month value, the component appends ?month=<session.month> to the destination URL. If session data is unavailable or month is not defined, it navigates to summer_camps.html without additional query parameters.

layout.grid_system: Centered fixed-width content column likely based on a 12-column grid (~1080–1200px wide) within full-bleed horizontal bands.
layout.layout_pattern: All camp-related components sit inside the main centered content column that appears below the global header stack (utility bar, main nav, page banner, and tabbed nav). Within this column, components are arranged in a single top-to-bottom sequence: a small back navigation link at the very top, followed by the camp session header (title and key facts), an optional inactive status banner, then the series of informational detail panels (schedule, fees, location, eligibility, activities), followed by messaging banners related to the interest form, then the interest form itself. No sidebars are used; any secondary details are integrated into the same column, occasionally right-aligned within the header row to echo the mockup’s small inline logo treatment.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: All camp-related components sit inside the main centered content column that appears below the global header stack (utility bar, main nav, page banner, and tabbed nav). Within this column, components are arranged in a single top-to-bottom sequence: a small back navigation link at the very top, followed by the camp session header (title and key facts), an optional inactive status banner, then the series of informational detail panels (schedule, fees, location, eligibility, activities), followed by messaging banners related to the interest form, then the interest form itself. No sidebars are used; any secondary details are integrated into the same column, occasionally right-aligned within the header row to echo the mockup’s small inline logo treatment.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The main content column is centered on the page and constrained to approximately 1080–1200px, occupying about 70–80% of the viewport width on desktop. Above this column, roughly the top 40% of the viewport height is consumed by the existing global bars (not part of these components). Within the camp page content, the header and key facts take the top 20–25% of the column’s visible area, the stacked detail panels fill the middle 40–50%, and the interest form plus its banners occupy the lower 25–35%. Each major block (header, each panel, form) is separated vertically with 24–32px of space, while internal spacing between text elements and controls follows the 8–16px rhythm.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout keeps a moderate content density: text lines are reasonably compact with comfortable line spacing, and most blocks use a single column to maintain readability. Each panel’s content generally spans 70–80% of the column width for text, avoiding overly long line lengths, with generous but not luxurious whitespace. Status banners and the form feel slightly denser, with tighter vertical spacing between related text and fields, but still adhere to the 8px spacing system to stay orderly and institutional.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows an F-pattern. Users first catch the global header and tabs, then drop into this page’s content at the top-left back link, move immediately down into the bold camp title in the header, scan right across any inline right-aligned metadata, then move down through the short key facts list. From there, the eye continues straight down through the stacked detail panels. Any status banner (inactive notice or form success/error) acts as a strong horizontal anchor that briefly interrupts this downward flow, drawing attention before the eye moves further down to the form fields. The back link at the top provides an early exit path consistent with breadcrumb behavior, while the form submit area at the bottom becomes the final focal point in the vertical scan.

layout.overall_description: Within the established site frame of stacked horizontal bars at the top of the page, the Summer Camp Detail content sits in a single, centered column below the tab strip. At the top-left of this column, a modest back link offers a way back to the list of camps, leading directly into a structured camp header where a large, left-aligned title and a compact right-aligned metadata block anchor the page. Just beneath, a concise bullet list of key facts mirrors the open-house mockup’s event summary. Below this header, any inactive notice appears as a full-width banner, followed by a vertical stack of clean, flat detail panels—schedule, fee, location, eligibility, and activities—each separated by consistent vertical spacing and using clear typographic hierarchy. Near the bottom, any confirmation or error banners for the interest form sit prominently across the content width, immediately above a single-column form with stacked labels and inputs. The whole layout feels like a practical, institutional information page: one main column, moderate spacing, consistent bars and headings, and an unhurried but efficient top‑to‑bottom reading experience.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When active, the loader appears as a semi-opaque overlay fixed over the entire viewport, sitting above all other elements including the global header bars. It slightly dims the page content below with a white or very light grey wash while keeping the underlying layout faintly visible. At the visual center of the screen (both horizontally and vertically), a small spinner or progress icon and a short status label (e.g., “Loading camp details…”) appear, aligned center, occupying roughly a 240–320px wide area. The overlay stretches edge-to-edge horizontally and vertically and does not scroll with the page, reinforcing that the whole page state is in loading.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed, full-viewport overlay that covers all other components while visible; non-scrolling and centered content within.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp session header occupies the top of the main content column, directly below the global tabs bar and just under the back-to-camps link. It spans the full width of the content column (approximately 1080–1200px centered on the page). At the very top of this component, there can be a subtle breadcrumb-style line or page context text aligned to the left, spanning about 70% of the column width. Below this, a large, bold H1 camp title sits left-aligned, occupying roughly the left 70–75% of the column. On the same horizontal row, aligned to the right edge, is a compact metadata block or small school-related mark (dates range, session code, or small school logo), taking up the remaining 25–30% of the width; it is vertically centered relative to the H1 baseline to echo the mockup’s right-aligned image behavior. Under the title row, a short bullet list of key session facts (e.g., dates, time, grades, daily schedule) runs in a single column, left-aligned, spanning about 70–80% of the column width. Vertical padding within the header is generous: approximately 24–32px above the title, 8–12px between the title row and the bullet list, and about 24px after the bullet list before the next component. The overall impression is a strong, top-heavy information header that anchors the page content and mirrors the clear hierarchy from the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static block element within the normal document flow, anchored to the top of the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The schedule panel is the first informational section directly beneath the header area (or beneath the inactive notice if present), separated by approximately 24–32px of vertical space. It spans the full width of the content column but keeps its textual content within a readable measure of about 70–80% width, aligned to the left, leaving a soft right margin that visually lightens the column. At the top of the panel, a clear H2 or section label (e.g., “Schedule”) is left-aligned and separated from the header above by a 24px gap. Below the heading, schedule details (dates, daily times, weekly breakdown) appear in concise paragraphs or a short list, with 8–12px vertical spacing between lines of information. The panel background remains the same as the page background (flat, minimal decoration), relying on typographic hierarchy and spacing rather than boxed styling, keeping with the conservative institutional feel. Bottom spacing of 24–32px separates this panel from the fee panel below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static section stacked in the vertical flow, expanding vertically to fit its content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The fee panel follows directly beneath the schedule panel, with 24–32px of vertical space separating the two. It also spans the full width of the content column, with its content text aligned left and constrained to roughly 70–80% of the column width. At the top, a left-aligned H2-level heading such as “Fees” marks the section. Below this heading, the cost details (base fee, additional charges, payment deadlines, discounts) are presented either as a short bullet list or compact paragraphs, each line separated by about 8–12px. Any critical notes (e.g., refund policy) can appear as a short emphasized line at the bottom of the section. The panel uses no heavy borders, maintaining a flat, text-focused look; vertical padding above and below the text matches adjacent panels to maintain rhythm. A 24–32px gap below separates it from the location panel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, single-column section in normal flow, with height determined by its content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The location panel sits below the fee panel, separated by a consistent 24–32px vertical gap, and maintains the full-width, single-column pattern. Its heading (e.g., “Location”) is left-aligned at the top, followed by the school name and address details. The main address text spans around 70–80% of the column width, left-aligned. If there is any map link or extra location note, it appears directly below the address as a short line, maintaining 8–12px spacing between lines. Overall vertical padding within this panel matches the schedule and fee panels, and another 24–32px gap is left below to separate it from the eligibility panel. The visual weight is similar to other detail panels so the information reads as part of a consistent, stacked set.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static section element in the linear flow, expanding vertically with text.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The eligibility panel appears directly below the location panel with 24–32px of vertical separation, continuing the single-column, full-width pattern. A left-aligned H2 heading such as “Eligibility” sits at the top, followed by bullet points or short paragraphs describing grade levels, age ranges, prerequisites, and any restrictions. The text content is kept within about 70–80% of the column width. Lines of text or bullets have 8–12px of spacing between them, and any particularly important constraints can be bolded within the lines without adding extra visual chrome. The panel ends with roughly 24–32px of vertical space before the activities panel. Its styling remains flat and typographic, in keeping with the institutional design language.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically stacking section of the main content column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The activities panel is the last of the informational sections and sits below the eligibility panel with a 24–32px gap. Like the previous panels, it spans the full width of the content column, with content constrained to approximately 70–80% width, left-aligned. The section begins with a clear H2 heading such as “Activities” followed by descriptive text or a bullet list of typical camp activities. If the content runs longer than other panels, it simply increases the panel’s height while maintaining the same internal spacing (8–12px between bullets or paragraphs). This panel forms the informational bridge between the factual details above and the interest form below, so there is a slightly more generous 32px of vertical space after its content before any form-related banners or the form itself, signaling a shift from information to action.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, terminal detail section in the stack, expanding vertically based on content length.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When the camp session is inactive or closed, this notice appears as a prominent, full-width banner directly below the camp-session-header and above the first detail panel. It spans the entire content column, edge-to-edge within that column, and is visually separated from the header by about 16–24px of vertical space, and from the next panel below by another 16–24px. The banner uses a slightly contrasting background (e.g., pale yellow or light grey) with a subtle border or left accent bar, and contains a concise status message aligned to the left. An icon (such as an info or warning symbol) may appear at the far left, with the text immediately to its right, all contained within an 8–16px internal padding. Text wraps within about 70–80% of the column width but the background color continues full-width for a strong horizontal band effect that echoes the mockup’s banners. This notice is visually strong but not as bold as the global nav bars above.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static banner in the normal flow; it pushes subsequent content down when present and leaves no empty gap when absent.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The camp interest form occupies the lower portion of the content column, starting approximately 32px below the activities panel or below any form-related banners if they are present above it. It spans the full width of the content column but keeps its internal content—form title, instructions, fields—within roughly 70–80% width, left-aligned. At the top of the form block, a clear H2 heading like “Parent Interest Form” is left-aligned, with a short explanatory line beneath it spaced by 8–12px. Below that, any validation summary component sits (if present), followed by the stacked form fields. Each field is presented in a single column: label on top, input control directly below, then vertical spacing of 12–16px before the next field. Related smaller fields (like phone and email) may appear one after another rather than side by side, maintaining the conservative, clarity-first style. The submit button row appears at the bottom left of the form, with 24px of space above it. The form as a whole has about 24–32px of padding at the top and bottom relative to surrounding components, and ends with a 32px gap before the bottom of the page content, preserving breathing room around this key interactive area.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, vertically expanding form within the main column; scrollable with the page and not fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The validation summary appears inside the camp-interest-form component, anchored near the top of the form content. It is positioned directly beneath the form heading and introductory text, and above the first fieldset, separated by about 16px above and 16px below. Visually, it spans the same width as the form’s inner content (about 70–80% of the content column) and is left-aligned. The summary is styled as a compact alert box: light background (e.g., pale red or light tint), a thin border or left accent bar in a warning color, and 8–12px of internal padding. A short title line (such as “Please correct the following”) is followed by a vertical list of error messages, each separated by 4–8px. The box does not extend beyond the form’s content width, so it feels integrated with the form rather than like an external banner.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static element within the form; appears only when there are validation errors, pushing fields downward but not affecting components above the form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: When the interest form is successfully submitted, the confirmation banner appears as a full-width bar within the main content column, just above the camp-interest-form’s heading area. It occupies the entire column width edge-to-edge and is separated from the activities panel or prior content by roughly 24px of vertical space. The banner uses a positive, lightly colored background (e.g., pale green or softly tinted blue) with 8–16px internal padding. Content is left-aligned, with an optional confirmation icon at the far left and one or two lines of confirmation text to its right, wrapped within about 70–80% of the column width. There is around 16–24px of vertical space between this banner and the top of the form, visually grouping them but keeping the banner distinct as a status strip, similar in visual grammar to system messages on institutional sites.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static banner in the normal flow that appears above the form when applicable, pushing the form content downward without overlapping.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error banner appears in the same location as the confirmation banner—just above the camp-interest-form’s heading and below the last informational panel—but only when a higher-level error occurs (e.g., submission failure). It spans the full width of the content column and is offset from the preceding content by about 24px. The banner uses an error-tinted background (e.g., pale red) with a subtle border or left accent, and 8–16px of padding inside. An error icon sits on the left, with a concise error message to its right, text-wrapped within about 70–80% of the column width. A 16–24px vertical gap separates this banner from the top of the form, visually coupling them as part of the same interaction context. Its styling is slightly more attention-grabbing than the validation summary box but consistent with the conservative, flat UI aesthetic.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width banner in the normal vertical flow; appears instead of or in addition to the confirmation banner depending on state, always above the form.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The back-to-camps link is positioned at the very top of the main content column, directly below the global tabbed navigation and above the camp-session-header. It is left-aligned and occupies only as much horizontal space as its text requires, creating a subtle breadcrumb-like cue at the top-left of the content area. There is approximately 16–24px of vertical space between this link and the header title below, giving it a clear but unobtrusive presence. The link may include a small left-pointing chevron icon followed by text (e.g., “Back to Summer Camps”), reinforcing its navigational nature while remaining visually light. It shares the same text style as secondary navigation links in the mockup (smaller size than headings, blue brand color), ensuring it does not compete with the main H1 for attention but is easy to spot on initial entry to the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static inline link at the top of the content column; scrolls with the page and does not stick to the viewport.
